steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says previous research found that species with a narrow range are more genetically homogeneous. M. anniana has a narrow range (Tamaulipas only). The simulation models what happens when M. anniana expands to new habitats — and the result must be "consistent with previous findings."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "there was a gradual increase in the genetic homogeneity of *Mammillaria* species in states neighboring Tamaulipas.": Wrong species and wrong direction. The question is about M. anniana, not neighboring-state Mammillaria species. Contradicts the pattern (wider range = less homogeneity, not more). ✗
  • "*Mammillaria* species other than *M. anniana* would become more common in Tamaulipas.": Addresses the wrong species entirely — nothing in the passage connects other Mammillaria species to M. anniana's range expansion. ✗
  • "several other *Mammillaria* species could survive in Tamaulipas in the future.": Again about other species, not M. anniana. Irrelevant to the simulation's focus on M. anniana's genetics as it spreads. ✗
  • "the genetic homogeneity of *M. anniana* decreased over time.": Correct species, correct direction. As M. anniana expands its range from narrow to wide, its genetic homogeneity decreases — exactly what the narrow-range = high-homogeneity finding predicts in reverse. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
